description: Bird was first seen on barbed wire fence. Flew off fence down to cattle pasture, where it was observed with prey. Tyrannus flycatcher with white head, slight dark mask, dark bill, with salmon pink wash on flanks and extremely long, forked tail. Bird showed pink underwing coverts in flight, white egdes to tails showed best in flight.Tail was  easily longer than the body of the bird, held stiffly. Note: tail was not long enough for male, therefore we think this was female.
